title: Law and justice
explanation:
Law and justice

  • verdict, evidence, defendant, prosecution, sentence
  • witness, jury, appeal, bail, conviction
  • verbs: sue, acquit, testify, convict

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The jury reached a guilty ____ after two days. | *verdict | platform | recipe
The ____ presented strong proof in court. | *prosecution | subscription | headline
The judge gave him a five-year ____. | *sentence | agenda | campaign
The ____ claimed he was at home when the crime happened. | *defendant | candidate | consumer

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: The ____ described exactly what she had seen. | witness
His lawyer filed an ____ against the decision. | appeal
The court decided to ____ him of all charges. | acquit
She was asked to ____ in front of the jury. | testify
The company threatened to ____ the newspaper for libel. | sue

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Without clear ____, the case may fail. | *evidence | publicity | furniture
The ____ must decide whether the accused is guilty. | *jury | audience | crowd
He was released on ____ until the next hearing. | *bail | debt | salary
A criminal ____ can affect future employment. | *conviction | vacation | discount
